Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally designated historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Municipally designated historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment

While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment

updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴式石室]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun |part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial_facility = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Ikeda City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Cultural Properties List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue ware fragment

It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue ware - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.
