Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment

updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |ISO = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated Artifacts - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == Artifacts == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] Artifacts recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |latitude = 34.823306 |longitude = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_artifacts = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |icon = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designated as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These artifacts are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City revision.comment: I hope this is not immediately undone

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated other_designation |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and has been other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was other_designation1 as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City other_designation on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == other_designation == === Ikeda City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural other_designation List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線}}{{Sfn|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City Cultural Property Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun Era in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]]
