(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment

Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

revision.comment: remarking some of the new pages
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} or other middle-ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|12=draft|12=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|12=draft|12=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:五月ヶ丘古墳 up to rev 99994931
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 五月ヶ丘古墳 |画像 = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
石室・陶棺（復元） |別名 = 五月ケ丘古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[池田市]][[五月丘]]1丁目
（五月丘緑地内） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 49|緯度秒 = 23.90 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 9.85 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径8m |埋葬施設 = 無袖式[[横穴式石室]]（内部に[[陶棺]]） |出土品 = [[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 池田市指定史跡「五月ヶ丘古墳及び出土遺物」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 五月ヶ丘
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''五月ヶ丘古墳'''（さつきがおかこふん、五月ケ丘古墳）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]][[五月丘]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。池田市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 大阪府北部、[[猪名川]]東岸の[[五月山]]丘陵南麓の尾根上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}}。これまでに墳丘は大きく削平を受けているほか、[[1973年|1973]]・[[1979年]]（[[昭和]]48・54年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|五月ヶ丘古墳|1980}}。 墳形は円形で、直径約8メートルを測る{{Sfn|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}}。埋葬施設は無袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長は3.5メートルを測り、石室内には長さ2メートルの[[陶棺]]が据えられる。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される{{Sfn|新修池田市史 第1巻|1997}}{{Sfn|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、[[秦氏]]一族（中間階層）とする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|新修池田市史 第1巻|1997}}史跡説明板。。 古墳域は[[1980年]]（昭和55年）に池田市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1955年]]（[[昭和]]30年）、古墳として発見{{Sfn|五月ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）、確認調査（大阪府教育委員会）{{Sfn|五月ヶ丘古墳|1980}}{{Sfn|五月ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）、池田市立歴史民俗資料館建設に伴う再調査、復元整備（池田市教育委員会、[[1980年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|五月ヶ丘古墳|1980}}{{Sfn|五月ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）3月24日、池田市指定史跡に指定。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陶棺}}{{small|[[池田市立歴史民俗資料館]]展示。}}]] 石室内からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}}。 * 陶棺 1 - 棺身のみ。家形四柱式で、復元長約2メートル。 * [[須恵器]] 1 - 追葬時の遺物か{{Sfn|五月ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 これらの出土品は、現在では池田市立歴史民俗資料館で保管されている。 File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 ファイル:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|須恵器 坏身
{{small|池田市立歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 池田市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 五月ヶ丘古墳及び出土遺物 - 1980年（昭和55年）3月24日指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の池田市ファイルより。。 == 交通アクセス == * 鉄道：[[阪急電鉄]][[阪急宝塚本線|宝塚本線]] [[池田駅 (大阪府)|池田駅]]（徒歩約15分）{{Sfn|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（池田市教育委員会、2001年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 五月ヶ丘古墳（池田市文化財調査報告 第3輯）]|publisher=池田市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref={{Harvid|五月ヶ丘古墳|1980}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=|title=新修池田市史 第1巻（地理・考古・古代・中世編）|publisher=池田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新修池田市史 第1巻|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=|title=古墳時代の猪名川流域 -猪名川流域に投影された畿内政権の動静-（池田市立歴史民俗資料館平成22年度特別展）|publisher=池田市立歴史民俗資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|古墳時代の猪名川流域|2010}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|year=1989|chapter=五月ケ丘古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|五月ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1967|chapter=|title=池田市史 史料編 第1（原始・古代・中世）|publisher=池田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|池田市史 史料編 第1|1967}}}} == 関連項目 == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{デフォルトソート:さつきかおかこふん}} [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:秦氏|墓さつきかおかこふん]] [[Category:五月丘]]
