{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuki Hill|qq=}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata clan|qq=}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment

Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyū Takarazuka Line|qq=}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the {{ill|Hata clan|ja|秦氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hata clan|qq=}} or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Ikeda Station (Osaka)|ja|池田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Station (Osaka)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Hankyū Takarazuka Line|ja|阪急宝塚本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyū Takarazuka Line|qq=}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuki Hill|qq=}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyū Takarazuka Line]]{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]]

Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyū Takarazuka Line]]{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:五月丘]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuki Hill|qq=}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyū Takarazuka Line]]{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:五月丘]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Satsukigaoka Kofun |image = [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|280px]]
Stone chamber and tōkan (restored) |alternate_name = Satsuki-gaoka Kofun | part_of = |location = [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan
(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuki Hill|qq=}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyū Takarazuka Line]]{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:五月丘]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]]

(within Satsukigaoka Green Space) |coordinates = 34.823306 |coordinates = 135.435292 |omitted = JP-27 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 8m |burial = Unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (with {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside) |excavated_burial goods = [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] |satellite_kofun = |built = 7th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Ikeda City Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Osaka |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Satsukigaoka
Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Satsukigaoka Kofun''' (五月ヶ丘古墳, Satsuki-gaoka Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] Satsukigaoka Kofun is situated in the northern part of Osaka Prefecture, on the southern slope of the {{ill|Satsuki Hill|ja|五月山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuki Hill|qq=}} ridge, along the eastern bank of the {{ill|Inagawa River|ja|猪名川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inagawa River|qq=}}. The mound has been significantly reduced due to erosion and human activity. Excavations were conducted in 1973 and 1979 during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}. The mound is circular, with a diameter of approximately 8 meters{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. Its burial facility is an unsleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is 3.5 meters long and contains a 2-meter-long {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. Based on its structure, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the 7th century{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. While the identity of the interred individual remains uncertain, some researchers suggest a connection to the [[:en:Hata clan|Hata clan]] or other middle-Ranking local elite groups{{Sfn|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}Historical explanation board.. The site was Historic Site1 as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24, 1980. == Archaeological History == * 1955: Discovered as a kofun{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1973: Initial investigation by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education{{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1979: Reinvestigation and restoration conducted during the construction of the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum (Ikeda City Board of Education, report published in 1980){{Sfn|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. * 1980: Designated as an Ikeda City Historic Site on March 24. == burial goods == [[File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tōkan}}{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}}]] burial goods recovered from the burial chamber include the following{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}: * Tōkan - Only the coffin base remains. It is a house-shaped structure supported by four pillars, with an estimated restored length of 2 meters. * Sue pottery - Possibly associated with secondary burials{{Sfn|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}. These burial goods are currently stored and displayed at the Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum. File:Satsukigaoka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view of the stone chamber File:五月ヶ丘古墳出土 須恵器 坏身.JPG|Sue pottery fragment
{{small|Exhibited at Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Ikeda City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Satsukigaoka Kofun and Associated burial goods - Designated on March 24, 1980[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefectural Historic Site List] (Osaka Prefecture official website, updated November 21, 2018).. == Access == * Rail: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Ikeda Station|Ikeda Station (Osaka)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyū Takarazuka Line]]{{Sfn|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|References used in the article}} * Historical explanation board (Ikeda City Board of Education, installed in 2001) * Local government publications ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1980|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/4854 Satsukigaoka Kofun (Ikeda City designation1 Survey No. 3)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Satsukigaoka Kofun|1980}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|title=New Ikeda City History, Volume 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient, and Medieval builts)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|New Ikeda City History, Volume 1|1997}}}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2010|title=Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin - Insights into Kinai Politics from the Inagawa River Basin|publisher=Ikeda Municipal History and Folklore Museum|ref={{Harvid|Kofun built in the Inagawa River Basin|2010}}}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Tetsu Takashima|year=1989|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=Tokyodō Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Satsuki-gaoka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Literature == {{small|Additional references not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1967|title=Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1 (Prehistoric, Ancient, and Medieval)|publisher=Ikeda City|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Historical Records, Volume 1|1967}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Satsukigaoka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|五月ヶ丘古墳|version=99994931|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Municipally Historic Site1 historic sites in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]][[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Satsukigaoka Kofun]] [[ja:五月ヶ丘古墳]] [[sn:atsukigaoka Kofun]] [[Category:五月丘]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:大阪府内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:秦氏]]
